summary: Maria Santos met with Derek Owens of Crestline Wealth to discuss their upcoming renewal in late March 2026, approximately six weeks out. They reviewed usage across three modules (Protect, Detect, and Comply), with Derek noting data growth from 14TB to approximately 17-17.5TB affecting tier pricing. Maria introduced an upcoming Comply multi-framework reporting feature and discussed pricing options ranging from $155K-$160K annually, with a two-year discount option available. The call ended with a clear plan: Maria will deliver a comparison quote and Detect resilience technical summary by February 27th, and Derek will provide a decision direction by March 5th.
actionItems: Maria Santos: Send a side-by-side renewal quote (1-year vs 2-year options) reflecting updated data volume by February 27th
Maria Santos: Provide a written technical summary of Detect pipeline resilience improvements by February 27th
Maria Santos: Pull and review the November/December backup failure support ticket to understand the delayed response
Derek Owens: Present renewal options to CFO and provide a decision or direction by March 5th

keyMoments.text: Derek emphasizes that reliability in threat monitoring is non-negotiable due to SEC and FINRA oversight, flagging a potential churn risk if Detect resilience is inadequate
keyMoments.speaker: Derek Owens

keyMoments.text: Maria reveals the upcoming Comply multi-framework on-demand reporting feature included at no additional cost, which Derek identifies as directly relevant to client demands
keyMoments.speaker: Maria Santos

keyMoments.text: Maria presents pricing in the $155K-$160K range (5-8% increase) and offers a two-year term discount to bring pricing closer to current contract value
keyMoments.speaker: Maria Santos

keyMoments.text: Derek discloses a November/December support ticket where a backup job failure took several days to resolve, expressing frustration with response time and lack of visibility
keyMoments.speaker: Derek Owens
